feedback.text: Good Evening, I dont really know how to add to this more than other commenters have. What I would like to say though, is that many Publishers nowadays mislead consumers by having you purchase Licenses instead of the actual game, which is not communicated properly to a buyer (Purchase-Button insinuating you bought the game, not a License). Games are an Important Media showing not only Art that would be lost, by killing games, but can also contain Criticism towards political or social issues. It is important that we preserve those Art Pieces for our children and people studying this Art Form or creating games with Inspiration from older Games. Nowadays Publishers are being unreasonable with their demands towards consumers. For Example: Ubisofts EULA says in Part 8. Termination, that not only should you deinstall games upon them stopping Support, but also destroy ANY Physical Copies of the now unsupported Game upon Sunset, that you may have in your possession. And Nintendo for example can Terminate (called Bricking) Internet support for a Nintendo Switch 2 at any given moment without having to give you a reason why, because they sold you a License to use a Switch 2, which is also not communicated properly. Not only has it happened to people using used games on their Switch 2, but also to people buying game codes on third party Websites. Consumers have NO choice, but to walk on eggshells in fear that they end up with a 500 Paperweight. In that regard Nintendo has Monopolized their own Digital Store (e-Shop) and The Brazilian Consumer Protection Agency has already opened up a Lawsuit against Nintendo and I hope, that the EU wont let consumers have to suffer under the awful EULAs and TOS of game companies, since the only thing consumers ask for are to: 1. Own their Games or have a reasonably playable game at the end of Publishers Live Service Support. 2. Not have to put up with everything the big Companies like Nintendo, Ubisoft, and the whole Mastercard Controversy, without having even rights to fight back, since many EULAs and TOSs have a clause in them saying you give up your right to sue them upon agreeing. 3. Have it communicated properly that you are buying a License instead of the Actual Game, that can be Terminated at any Moment without giving you a reason nor a Refund. We want to preserve this medium and this Form of Art for the upcoming generations, but it is hard for us consumers, since many companies seem to act against consumers and their are no proper Laws and Regulations in Place to Protect us or the following generations of consumers and players.
